notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.
notes.contentHtml:
THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below.


Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion.


For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors, visit the UCSC Admissions website.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: Transfer students who want to pursue the computer engineering major, must have applied and been admitted to UC Santa Cruz as a proposed computer engineering major. Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of at least SIX of the courses listed in the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below, with a cumulative GPA of 2.8 or better, no later than the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall. All attempted screening courses are included in the cumulative GPA (note: lecture and lab combinations count as one course). Courses completed via exam credit will count toward the minimum six courses required but are not included in the minimum GPA. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation Students who wish to graduate in two years are strongly recommended to complete all lower-division major requirements before coming to UC Santa Cruz. To earn their degree within two years after transferring, it is recommended that transfer students have completed courses transferable as PHYS 5A/PHYS 5L, PHYS 5C/PHYS 5N, MATH 19A, MATH 19B, and one of the following: MATH 23A, AM 10, MATH 21, or AM 20. In addition, completion of at least one programming course is recommended. Additional Information for Transfer Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or as many UC Santa Cruz general education requirements as possible before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Visit the Baskin Engineering Undergraduate Experience website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Computer Engineering B.S. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
Transfer students who want to pursue the computer engineering major, must have applied and been admitted to UC Santa Cruz as a proposed computer engineering major.


Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of at least SIX of the courses listed in the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below, with a cumulative GPA of 2.8 or better, no later than the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall.


All attempted screening courses are included in the cumulative GPA (note: lecture and lab combinations count as one course). Courses completed via exam credit will count toward the minimum six courses required but are not included in the minimum GPA.


Strongly Recommended Major Preparation

Students who wish to graduate in two years are strongly recommended to complete all lower-division major requirements before coming to UC Santa Cruz. To earn their degree within two years after transferring, it is recommended that transfer students have completed courses transferable as PHYS 5A/PHYS 5L, PHYS 5C/PHYS 5N, MATH 19A, MATH 19B, and one of the following: MATH 23A, AM 10, MATH 21, or AM 20. In addition, completion of at least one programming course is recommended.


Additional Information for Transfer

Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or as many UC Santa Cruz general education requirements as possible before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.



Visit the Baskin Engineering Undergraduate Experience website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Computer Engineering B.S. Program Statement
